metadata.task_description: Architectural analysis of the antfarm multi-agent workflow orchestration system

architecture_patterns: Plugin backend system (Backend interface with 4 implementations: OpenClaw, Hermes, Claude Code, Codex)
Strategy pattern for backend selection (config-resolver priority: CLI > agent > workflow > global > default)
Subprocess-based scheduling (SubprocessScheduler polls SQLite for pending steps, spawns claude/codex processes)
Cron-driven orchestration for OpenClaw/Hermes (gateway API creates per-agent cron jobs)
Polling model with step state machine (pending -> running -> done/failed/blocked/retry)
Story-based loop execution (loop steps iterate over stories with fresh sessions per iteration)
Event sourcing for observability (JSONL events file with webhook forwarding)
Health watchdog (Medic) for stuck-step detection and auto-remediation

coding_conventions.module_system: ESM (type: module in package.json, .js extensions in imports)
coding_conventions.error_handling: try-catch with graceful degradation, logging never throws, best-effort for fire-and-forget operations

tech_stack.database: SQLite via node:sqlite (DatabaseSync, synchronous API, WAL mode, migration-based schema evolution)
tech_stack.cli_framework: Custom CLI in src/cli/cli.ts (no framework, manual arg parsing)
tech_stack.http_server: node:http (raw HTTP for dashboard, no Express/Fastify)

modules.name: src/backend/
modules.role: Plugin backend system - 4 agent runtime backends
key_files: interface.ts (Backend interface: install/uninstall/startRun/stopRun)
index.ts (factory: createBackend, re-exports)
config-resolver.ts (5-level priority resolution for backend selection)
group-agents.ts (groups workflow agents by resolved backend type)
openclaw.ts (OpenClawBackend: cron-based, ROLE_POLICIES with per-tool deny lists)
hermes.ts (HermesBackend: profile-per-agent, hermes CLI wrapping)
claude-code.ts (ClaudeCodeBackend: project .claude/ artifacts, no-op startRun/stopRun)
claude-code-install.ts (subagent definition .md files with frontmatter)
claude-code-spawn.ts (buildClaudeCodeArgv for -p subprocess)
claude-code-policy.ts (ROLE_DISALLOWED_TOOLS per role)
codex.ts (CodexBackend: global ~/.codex/ artifacts, TOML config block management)
codex-config.ts (marker-based TOML block rewriting without parser dep)
codex-policy.ts (sandbox modes + developer_instructions guardrails)
codex-spawn.ts (buildCodexExecArgv for codex exec subprocess)

modules.name: src/installer/
modules.role: Workflow lifecycle management - install, run, step execution, uninstall
key_files: step-ops.ts (1103 lines - step state machine, claim/complete/fail, loop handling, output parsing)
agent-cron.ts (polling prompt construction, role guardrails, cron teardown)
gateway-api.ts (422 lines - OpenClaw gateway REST API client)
uninstall.ts (405 lines - workflow/agent cleanup, active-run guards)
install.ts (orchestration: fetch workflow spec, group by backend, install serially)
run.ts (create run records, initialize steps/stories)
workflow-spec.ts (YAML workflow parser, template resolution)
workflow-fetch.ts (bundled workflow discovery and copy)
agent-provision.ts (agent workspace provisioning)
skill-install.ts (SKILL.md template substitution for each backend)
events.ts (JSONL event log with rotation and webhook forwarding)
status.ts (run/step status queries)
paths.ts (path resolution: antfarm root, workflows, workspaces, runs)
types.ts (core types: WorkflowSpec, WorkflowAgent, WorkflowStep, Story, AgentRole)

modules.name: src/server/
key_files: subprocess-scheduler.ts (566 lines - SubprocessScheduler class, polls DB, spawns claude/codex, reaps on exit)
dashboard.ts (149 lines - raw HTTP API server, serves runs/steps/workflows/events/medic data)
daemon.ts (30 lines - entry point: starts scheduler + dashboard, PID file, signal handling)
daemonctl.ts (daemon lifecycle: start/stop/status)

modules.name: src/cli/
key_files: cli.ts (770 lines - all CLI subcommands: install, uninstall, run, status, tick, daemon, medic, etc.)
ant.ts (top-level entry point)

modules.name: src/lib/
key_files: config.ts (global config.yaml read/write at ~/.config/antfarm/)
logger.ts (rotating file logger at ~/.openclaw/antfarm/logs/)
frontend-detect.ts (detects frontend file changes for conditional visual verification)

modules.name: src/medic/
modules.role: Health watchdog - detects and remediates stuck/stalled workflow state
key_files: medic.ts (280 lines - runs periodic checks, auto-remediates stuck steps, orphaned crons)
checks.ts (210 lines - modular check functions: stuckSteps, orphanedCrons)
medic-cron.ts (cron-based medic scheduling)

project_context.test_coverage: 31+ test files across src/ (unit co-located) and tests/ (integration). Tests cover: polling model, cron payload, external skills, gateway API, frontend detection/e2e, logger callers, model field preservation, multiline output parsing, peek-step polling, terminal state guards, two-phase cron/integration, work prompts, workflow skills, security audit polling, and backend-specific tests (hermes, codex, claude-code, openclaw, config-resolver, group-agents). Uses tsx --test with node:test runner.

core_modules.role: Core type definitions (WorkflowSpec, WorkflowAgent, WorkflowStep, Story, AgentRole)
Step state machine - claim/complete/fail, loop iteration, output parsing
Scheduler - polls DB for pending steps, spawns claude/codex, manages concurrency
SQLite database (node:sqlite sync), schema migration, connection caching
OpenClaw backend - cron jobs, ROLE_POLICIES, per-tool deny lists
Claude Code backend - project .claude/ artifacts, subagent definitions
Codex backend - global ~/.codex/ artifacts, TOML config block management
Hermes backend - profile-per-agent, CLI wrapping for profile creation/cron
Polling prompt construction, role guardrails, cron teardown

config_files.notes: Runtime deps: json5, yaml. Dev: @types/node, typescript. Node >= 22. ESM.
ES2022, NodeNext modules, strict mode, rootDir=src

workflow_definitions.role: Feature development workflow (7 agents, 7 steps, loop-based story execution)

tests.notes: Integration tests covering polling, cron, gateway, frontend, logger, model preservation, terminal states, two-phase

Co-located unit tests for backend (hermes, codex, claude-code, openclaw, config-resolver, group-agents, claude-code-spawn, codex-spawn, claude-code-policy, codex-policy, codex-config), installer (status, run, skill-install, workflow-spec)

runtime.usage: YAML parsing for workflow.yml and config.yaml
Synchronous SQLite database for runs/steps/stories state
File system operations (workspace provisioning, config, logs)
Home directory resolution for state paths

existing_implementations: src/installer/step-ops.ts (1103 lines - largest file, complex state machine)
src/cli/cli.ts (770 lines - manual arg parsing, fragile for new subcommands)

potential_issues.mitigation: Consider extracting loop-handling or output-parsing into separate modules

potential_issues.issue: cli.ts at 770 lines with manual arg parsing is fragile for extension
potential_issues.mitigation: Manual parsing works but adding subcommands requires careful positional/flag ordering

potential_issues.issue: Synchronous SQLite API (DatabaseSync) blocks the event loop during DB operations
potential_issues.mitigation: Acceptable for current scale; WAL mode mitigates contention. Would need async refactor for high concurrency

potential_issues.issue: DB connection caching with 5-second TTL (DB_MAX_AGE_MS) may cause stale connections
potential_issues.mitigation: Reconnection logic exists, but rapid sequential calls may open/close repeatedly

potential_issues.issue: Hermes CLI flag positioning inconsistencies documented in CLAUDE.md
potential_issues.mitigation: Well-documented gotchas, but new contributors may hit these

potential_issues.issue: Marker race condition (.antfarm marker written after profile creation)
potential_issues.mitigation: Acknowledged in CLAUDE.md as known limitation, not yet fixed

conflict_detection.mitigation_strategy: Analysis-only task, no modifications planned. Key files identified for reference.

architecture_diagram.data_flow: CLI (cli.ts) -> Installer (install.ts) -> Backend (interface.ts implementations) -> Scheduler (subprocess-scheduler.ts) -> Subprocess (claude -p / codex exec / hermes cron) -> DB (db.ts) -> Dashboard (dashboard.ts)
architecture_diagram.state_machine: run: running -> paused/blocked/completed/canceled | step: pending -> running -> done/failed/waiting | story: pending -> running -> done/failed
architecture_diagram.loop_execution: step type=loop -> iterates over stories -> fresh session per iteration -> optional verify_each with verify_step
